facts.description: R1 rate limiting formula explicitly references R3 backpressure_high_watermark: rate = base_rate_limit * (1 - current_queue_depth / backpressure_high_watermark) where base_rate_limit = 10,000 events/sec. If R1 and R3 are split into separate releases, the formula loses the concrete value it depends on.

T1 retry flow has 6 distinct steps: immediate retry (0ms), then 1000ms, 2000ms, 4000ms, 8000ms, then critical_failure_handler which does THREE things: pages on-call AND writes to DLQ AND emits critical_event_failed alert. All three actions on the 6th attempt must be preserved.

Classification rule 4 contains an EXCLUSION: account.* matches lifecycle/T2 EXCEPT account.locked which is matched by Rule 2 as security/T1. First-match-wins ordering is critical — if rules are reordered or split, account.locked may be misclassified.
facts.source_section: R2: Classification rules and Critical classification constraint

facts.description: T4 events MUST NOT appear in the DLQ under any circumstances. On failure T4 events are discarded (not DLQ'd). If found in DLQ it indicates a classification or routing bug. The DLQEntry schema has tier: Literal['T1', 'T2', 'T3'] — T4 is explicitly excluded from the type.
facts.source_section: R4: T4 retry flow and R6: DLQ entry schema

facts.description: T1 eviction cascade is strictly T4 then T3 then REJECT. It MUST NOT extend to T2. A T1 event that cannot find T4 or T3 to evict MUST be rejected (HTTP 503) rather than evicting a T2 event. This prevents T1 bursts from starving T2.
facts.source_section: R3: Critical constraint on T1 eviction behavior

facts.description: Error classification check MUST happen BEFORE retry attempt. permanent_error on first attempt MUST skip ALL retries and proceed directly to failure disposition. Retry budget is ONLY consumed by transient_error failures. This prevents INC-063 pattern.

Three event failure destinations are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: rejected_events (failed ingestion validation), DLQ (passed validation, failed processing, T1-T3), poison_events (handler crash), discarded (T4 only). An event ends in exactly one of: successfully processed, rejected_events, DLQ, poison_events, or discarded.
Startup sequence has 6 steps in EXACT order. Step 6 (open ingestion) MUST NOT execute before step 3 (handler health check). Accepting events before handlers healthy caused INC-063 variant.
facts.source_section: R8: Startup sequence and Critical startup constraint

facts.description: Shutdown step 3 returns events to queue HEAD (not tail) for reprocessing. Head-insertion MUST be atomic with queue state persistence in step 6. If crash between steps 3 and 6, WAL from last checkpoint is used and in-flight events recovered from handler idempotency log.

R9 ordering interaction with R4 retry: when financial/security/configuration event retries, ALL subsequent same-entity events are BLOCKED. Blocking bounded by tier retry window from R4 (60s T1, 30s T2). Blocked events do NOT consume their own retry budget — timer starts only at handler dispatch.
facts.source_section: R9: Critical ordering interaction with R4 retry

facts.description: Rejected events go to rejected_events topic, NOT the DLQ. DLQ is reserved for events that PASSED validation but FAILED during processing.

Backpressure thresholds: low_watermark=20,000 events, high_watermark=80,000 events, critical_watermark=95,000 events. Queue max depth=100,000. These exact values are referenced by R1 rate formula and R3 shedding levels.

Backpressure shedding varies by level AND tier: yellow sheds T4 only; red sheds T3+T4; critical sheds T2+T3+T4. At red level, T3 shed events go to shed_events topic. At critical level, T2 shed events go to BOTH shed_events AND deferred_processing queue for auto-replay when green returns.

T1 retry delay formula: delay_ms = 2^(attempt-2) * 1000 for attempt >= 2, with delay_ms = 0 for attempt 1. Total retry window 60 seconds. If window expires, remaining retries ABANDONED and event goes directly to critical_failure_handler.

Poison events (handler crash/unhandled exception) are NOT retryable and go to poison_events topic (not DLQ). This is DISTINCT from DLQ. Poison events include handler crash dump. This prevents INC-063 pattern where poison events consumed full retry budget.

Handler supported_event_types() returns frozenset, MUST be frozen at registration time. Modification after registration MUST be rejected with exact error: 'HandlerRegistrationError: Event type set is immutable after registration'. Prevents INC-078 class of bugs.

Handler pool worker counts are IMMUTABLE at runtime (requires restart). But backpressure thresholds, retry delays, cache TTL, alerting thresholds, and grace periods are MUTABLE at runtime. This distinction between mutable and immutable config MUST be preserved.

DLQ disposition is tier-specific with exact rules: T1=requires_immediate_attention (PagerDuty, auto-retry 5min, max 3 DLQ retries), T2=requires_manual_review (no auto-retry, 30-day retention), T3=auto_retry_eligible (batch retry 15min, max 10 DLQ retries, promotes to T2 handling after 10 fails).

Classification cache TTL is 300 seconds (5 minutes), NOT infinite. On rule change ALL cached entries MUST be invalidated immediately (not on next TTL expiry). Cache max 10,000 entries, LRU eviction. INC-078 root cause was infinite TTL.

T2 retry has 3 retries with specific delays: 2000ms, 5000ms, 10000ms. Fourth attempt fails writes to DLQ with disposition requires_manual_review. T2 DLQ entries MUST include original payload, all attempt timestamps, and failure reason for each attempt.

Ordering guarantees vary per category: financial=strict FIFO per entity_id, security=strict FIFO AND no concurrent same-entity processing, lifecycle=best-effort FIFO (reorder during retry allowed), telemetry=no ordering, configuration=strict FIFO, unclassified=no ordering.

Handler degradation threshold: >20% of events exceeding SLA in 5-minute window triggers handler_pool_degraded alert. Degraded pool continues processing but routes through shadow_handler in parallel for comparison.

DLQ capacity 50,000 entries. At 90% (45,000) emit dlq_capacity_warning alert. At 100% oldest T3 auto_retry_eligible evicted. T1/T2 NEVER evicted from DLQ. If no T3 and DLQ full, overflow file written with dlq_overflow alert.

Configuration change audit requires minimum 10 character reason. Changes without reason rejected with exact error: 'ConfigChangeError: Reason must be at least 10 characters (got {len})'. Must log parameter_name, old_value, new_value, changed_by, timestamp_utc, change_reason.

Graceful shutdown max duration is exactly shutdown_grace_period + drain_timeout + 10 = 30 + 300 + 10 = 340 seconds. Grace period (30s) is for in-flight events (step 2). Drain timeout (300s/5min) is for queued-but-not-started events. These are SEPARATE timeouts with distinct purposes.
facts.source_section: R8: Drain timeout escalation and Section 6 NFR
